title: rubus tereticaulis subsp. bollae
intro: Rubus tereticaulis subsp. bollae, also known as Boll's bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus tereticaulis species, a type of flowering raspberry plant native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Rubus tereticaulis subsp. bollae include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
